title: Phishing Campaign Hits 80+ Orgs Using SimpleHelp and ScreenConnect RMM Tools
summary: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025, with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remote access to compromised hosts.
card_summary: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025, with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remote access to compromised hosts.
quick_take: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025, with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remote access to…

sections.context:
This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

This fits the broader data-exposure trend where follow-on abuse of leaked information is common.

email_summary: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025, with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remote access to compromised hosts. The activity, codenamed VENOMOUS#HELPER, has impacted over 80 organizations, most of which are in the U.S., according to Securonix.

title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
summary: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass.
card_summary: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass.

sections.impact:
MOVEit Automation (formerly Central) is a secure, server-based managed file transfer (MFT) solution used to schedule and automate file movement workflows in enterprise environments without requiring any custom scripts.

Assessed severity: HIGH.

email_summary: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass. Severity is rated high.

title: Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
summary: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches.
card_summary: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches.

sections.technical: While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open doors, and open-source pipelines into silent delivery systems.

title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
summary: Shortly after the authentication-bypass flaw was disclosed multiple proof-of-concept exploits appeared, and one researcher claims there's been zero-day activity for at least a month.

title: 2026: The Year of AI-Assisted Attacks
summary: On December 4, 2025, a 17-year-old was arrested in Osaka under Japan’s Unauthorized Access Prohibition Act. The young man had run malicious code to extract the personal data of over 7 million users of Kaikatsu Club, Japan's largest internet cafe chain. When asked, the young man shared his motivation for the hack: he wanted to buy Pokémon cards.

title: Silver Fox Deploys ABCDoor Malware via Tax-Themed Phishing in India and Russia
summary: The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor.
card_summary: The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor.

sections.context: This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

email_summary:
The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor. The activity involved using phishing emails that mimic correspondence from the Income Tax Department of India in December 2025, followed by a similar campaign aimed at Russian entities.

title: Critical cPanel Vulnerability Weaponized to Target Government and MSP Networks
summary: A previously unknown threat actor has been observed targeting government and military entities in Southeast Asia, alongside a smaller cluster of managed service providers (MSPs) and hosting providers in the Philippines, Laos, Canada, South Africa, and the U.S., by exploiting the recently disclosed vulnerability in cPanel.

title: Global Crackdown Arrests 276, Shuts 9 Crypto Scam Centers, Seizes $701M
summary: A coordinated international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities has arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine scam centers used for cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes targeting Americans, resulting in millions of dollars in losses.
